ogTitle: Jack River vs Jackass Flat: Climate Safety

heading: Jack River vs Jackass Flat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jack River, Victoria and Jackass Flat, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jackass Flat has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Jack River (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jack River or Jackass Flat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jack River has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Jackass Flat scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jack River scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Jackass Flat scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jack River or Jackass Flat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jackass Flat has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
